concept_seed.one_liner: A niche-sport governing body website for a national curling federation with icy blue-and-granite palette, utilitarian sans typography, dense league standings tables, athlete roster pages, tournament brackets, club locator map, rules/officiating handbook section, news ticker, and a results archive with filter chips.

metadata.palette: cool icy blue, granite gray, white

accepted_concept.domain: National sport governing body — curling federation
accepted_concept.site_goal: Serve as the authoritative operational portal for a national curling federation: publish league standings, athlete rosters, tournament brackets, results archives, officiating rules, and a club locator for member clubs and competitive curlers.
audience: Recreational players looking for a club
accepted_concept.description: A dense, utilitarian institutional site for the National Curling Federation. The aesthetic reads like a federation handbook crossed with a live scoreboard: icy blue (#BCD7E6) panels, granite gray (#3A3F45) headers, and crisp white data tables. Sans-serif typography (a workhorse grotesk) sits at tight leading, with monospaced numerals in standings columns. A persistent results ticker scrolls along the top below the masthead. The homepage stacks a national rankings widget, the next-up tournament bracket teaser, and a 'club of the week' card over a low-contrast topographic ice-rink line motif. Internal pages favor multi-column tables, filter chip rows, and small-cap metadata labels. Iconography is line-only: brooms, stones, hammer indicators, and circular target house diagrams used as section bullets.
accepted_concept.motif: Granite stone cross-section circles, broom-stroke underlines, and concentric target-house rings used as accents around stat blocks; thin hairline rules separate dense table rows.

pages.layout_pattern: Masthead with federation crest left, primary nav row, scrolling results ticker bar, then a three-column dense dashboard: national rankings table (left), upcoming tournament bracket preview (center), latest news + officiating bulletins (right); below, a club-of-the-week spotlight and sponsor strip footer.
sections: Federation masthead with crest and tagline
National rankings table — men's, women's, mixed doubles tabs
Footer with affiliation, contact, and member portal link

pages.layout_pattern: Full-width data table page: filter chip bar at top (season, division, region, gender, discipline), pinned column headers, sortable columns, alternating row shading; sidebar showing legend (W/L, DSC, hammer %), and a small inline trendline sparkline per team.

Roster directory: grid of athlete cards with portrait, club, position (skip/vice/second/lead), national team caps; left-rail filters by team, region, hand dominance, age category; click-through to an athlete profile sub-page with career stats table and competition history timeline.

Horizontal single-elimination bracket diagram spanning a wide scrollable canvas with round-robin pool tables stacked above; each match cell shows team names, end-by-end score grid, hammer indicator, and ice sheet assignment.
sections: Tournament header with dates, venue, draw status

pages.layout_pattern: Two-pane locator: interactive national map (left, ~60%) with dot markers shaded by club size, and a right-side scrollable result list with club name, city, sheets count, and contact; chip filters above for league type, learn-to-curl availability, accessibility, wheelchair curling.
sections: Filter chips (junior program, accessibility, league nights, leagues offered)

pages.layout_pattern: Documentation-style two-pane layout: sticky left-rail table of contents (collapsible chapters), main column with numbered sections, inline diagrams of the house and free-guard zone, callout boxes for rule changes, footnote citations to WCF references.
sections: Handbook version banner with revision date
Diagrams: house, hog line, free guard zone

pages.layout_pattern: Archive page with prominent filter chip rail (season range slider, event type, gender, region, medal-only toggle), then a dense paginated results table grouped by event with expandable rows revealing end-by-end scores; right-side facet panel of years.
sections: Grouped results table with expandable end-by-end detail

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial index: lead story hero with overline category tag, then a two-column list of dated bulletins (selection news, sanctions, results recaps), with a sidebar 'Officiating notices' feed and an athlete-of-the-month box.

message_intent: Convey institutional authority and procedural rigor
Surface live and historical competition data quickly
Make it easy to find a local club and join a league
content_model: Athlete (name, club, position, caps, career_stats, hand)
Club (name, city, region, sheets, programs, contact, geo)
Tournament (event, dates, venue, draws, brackets, officials)
interactions: Sort and filter dense standings tables via chip rows
Pan/zoom the club locator map and click markers for a flyout
Expand match rows to reveal end-by-end scoring grids
Filter results archive by season range slider
Subscribe to officiating notices via email
Download handbook PDF and CSV result exports
asset_needs: National map vector with regional boundaries
accepted_concept.mobile_behavior: Tables collapse into stacked key-value cards with sticky filter chip drawer; bracket scrolls horizontally with a minimap; club locator switches to a list-first view with a map toggle button at top.

strengths: Eight concrete pages fully aligned with the seed (standings, athletes, brackets, clubs, rules handbook, results archive, news, home)
Palette adheres strictly to seed: icy blue, granite gray, white — no off-spec accents
Motif is specific and curling-native: granite cross-sections, broom-stroke underlines, concentric house rings as section bullets
Interactions are dense and useful: chip filters, expandable end-by-end rows, season range slider, bracket view toggle
Content model captures real curling primitives (hammer_pct, end_scores, sheet assignment, officials levels)
Layout patterns are concrete with column counts, sticky headers, sparklines, and sidebar legends
Mobile behavior is reasoned per page (tables→stacked cards, bracket minimap, list-first club view)
weaknesses: Home dashboard could specify more numerical density (explicit column/row counts)
Athlete profile sub-page is implied but not enumerated as its own page entry
Sponsor strip risks looking generic if not handled with restraint

strengths: Eight pages with strong domain specificity (LiveMatch entity, advanced stats columns DSC/ENDS±/HAM%/DPI, head-to-head matrix)
Left-rail function taxonomy (Compete/Officiate/Develop/Watch/Records) reads like a real federation IA
Champions wall, sanctions & eligibility sidebar, and broadcast embed slot add realism
Pebbled-ice halftone and scoreboard segmented digits are distinctive motifs
Mobile behavior handles left-rail collapse and bracket swipe explicitly
weaknesses: Introduces a hot brick-red accent outside the seed palette (cool icy blue, granite gray, white only)
'Broadcast control room' framing slightly undercuts the seed's 'utilitarian institutional' tone
Some redundancy between league-table and results-archive faceting
Live broadcast embed slot may be ambitious for a static concept render

issues.message: Search input and filter checkboxes appear to lack associated labels or accessible names.
Search input and possibly the map/filter controls are missing accessible names or form labels.
Newsletter or filter input is missing an associated label or accessible name.
Three-column dashboard rows show some bottom-edge misalignment between the rankings, bracket, and news columns.
Athlete cards have several rows where card bottoms do not align cleanly — likely due to variable text length in the stat blocks.
Bulletin rows in the two-column list have inconsistent bottom alignment.

repair_instructions: On athletes.html, add a visible or visually-hidden <label> tied to the surname/club search input via for/id, and ensure each discipline/position/region filter checkbox has an associated <label> element (or aria-label) so screen readers announce them.
On clubs.html, add a label or aria-label to the 'Search clubs, cities, postal codes…' input and to any map control buttons (zoom, pan toggles) that are currently icon-only.

On news.html, add a label or aria-label to the 'Subscribe to officiating notices' email input (and any category/format filter controls that are icon- or chip-only without text labels for assistive tech).

On index.html, equalize the heights of the three dashboard columns (National Rankings, Playoff Bracket, News & Notices) — e.g. use a CSS grid with align-items: stretch and give the inner card bodies min-height or flex: 1 so their bottom rules line up at the same y-coordinate.
On athletes.html, give every athlete card a uniform min-height (or use grid auto-rows: 1fr within each row) so the card bottoms align across the row even when stat blocks have differing line counts.
On news.html, give the bulletin rows in the two-column list a fixed or min-height so the bottom rules and metadata rows align horizontally between the left and right columns.

deterministic_checks.page_presence_resolution: All 8 declared pages resolve to concrete files. The two paths flagged as unresolved by the extractor (/officiating/rules-handbook and /tournaments/national-championship/bracket) are clearly implemented as rules.html and brackets.html — this is a path-mapping artifact, not a missing page.
